title: Mesembryanthemum formosulum
taxonomy: Mesembryanthemum formosulum is a species of flowering plant in the ice plant family, Aizoaceae. It is native to South Africa. The classification of Mesembryanthemum formosulum is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, order Caryophyllales, family Aizoaceae, genus Mesembryanthemum, and species M. formosulum. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants. Further research may provide more insight into the classification and taxonomy of Mesembryanthemum formosulum.
uses: Mesembryanthemum formosulum, also known as the "ice plant," has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's extracts have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Its leaves are also used to reduce inflammation and ease pain. Additionally, the plant's sap is believed to have antiseptic properties, helping to heal wounds and prevent infection.

preparations: Mesembryanthemum formosulum, also known as the Formosa iceplant or Formosa living rock, is a succulent species native to southern Africa. There is limited information available on the traditional or modern herbal preparations of this species.
